region_of_origin: New Zealand (commercially dominant cultivar); originally China

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium green kiwi, peeled, weighs approximately 76 g and provides around 46 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Kiwifruit, green, raw — Singapore HPB Foodo / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 09148)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) and USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database (data_source: hpb, confidence 0.95). Cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for raw green kiwifruit (FDC ID 168153) to confirm macro and micronutrient values. Saturated fat sourced from USDA SR Legacy. Values are consistent across both approved sources.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Kiwifruit, Zespri Green
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.url: https://www.glycemicindex.com/; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 52 (±6) for green kiwifruit (Zespri Green) is directly reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and confirmed in the peer-reviewed International Tables of GI and GL (Brand-Miller et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2002; Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008). GL per serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving / 100 = 52 × (14.66 × 0.76) / 100 = 52 × 11.14 / 100 ≈ 5.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (3 mg/100g) and high potassium (312 mg/100g) with a favourable Na:K ratio
hypertension.guidance: Green kiwi's very low sodium content and high potassium (312 mg/100g) support blood pressure reduction; it is well-suited to a DASH dietary pattern and can be consumed freely as part of a balanced diet for hypertensive individuals.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (52) modulated by 3.0 g/100g dietary fibre slowing glucose absorption
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 52 and 3.0 g dietary fibre per 100g, green kiwi produces a modest glycaemic response; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes may include 1–2 fruits per serving while monitoring total carbohydrate intake and post-meal glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High vitamin C (92.7 mg/100g), dietary fibre (3.0 g/100g), potassium, and negligible saturated fat (0.03 g/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Green kiwi's high vitamin C, dietary fibre, and potassium content combined with negligible saturated fat and zero cholesterol make it a cardioprotective fruit that supports endothelial function and lipid management in individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High vitamin C (92.7 mg/100g) and polyphenols with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Green kiwi's high vitamin C and polyphenol content provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; consuming fresh fruit (rather than juice) preserves fibre and maximises antioxidant delivery.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Green kiwi (Actinidia deliciosa) is a fresh whole fruit characterised by a brown, fuzzy exterior and vibrant bright-green flesh studded with small black seeds. It has a sweet-tart flavour profile with a soft, juicy texture. Commercially, the Zespri Green cultivar is the most widely recognised variety. Per 100g, green kiwi provides 61 kcal, 1.14g protein, 0.52g fat (of which only 0.03g is saturated), and 14.66g carbohydrate with 8.99g sugars and 3.0g dietary fibre. It is an exceptional source of vitamin C (92.7 mg/100g, exceeding the adult RDI in a single medium fruit), and also provides potassium (312 mg), calcium (34 mg), folate (25 mcg DFE), and vitamin E (1.46 mg). Its glycaemic index is 52 (low GI), making it suitable for blood glucose management. The high potassium-to-sodium ratio (312:3) is strongly supportive of blood pressure control. Dietary fibre at 3.0g/100g supports gut health and slows glucose absorption. Green kiwi is vegan, gluten-free, halal, and kosher. It is commonly consumed fresh as a snack, in fruit salads, smoothies, or as a topping for yoghurt and desserts. Clinically, it is appropriate for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease, and its anti-inflammatory micronutrient profile is beneficial for rheumatoid arthritis.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a green kiwi?
What is the GI of kiwi?
Is kiwi good for blood pressure?
How much vitamin C is in kiwi?
Can I eat kiwi if I have hypertension?
What are the health benefits of green kiwi?

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (confidence 0.95, reviewed status) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for raw green kiwifruit. GI value of 52 is directly reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature. All core macros and key micronutrients are supported by named approved sources.
notes: Saturated fat (0.03g), monounsaturated fat (0.05g), and polyunsaturated fat (0.29g) sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 168153) as HPB record does not disaggregate fatty acid fractions. Vitamin C (92.7 mg), vitamin A (4 mcg RAE), B-vitamins, vitamin D (0.0g — confirmed negligible in raw kiwi per USDA), vitamin E (1.46 mg), phosphorus (34 mg), magnesium (17 mg), zinc (0.14 mg), selenium (0.2 mcg), and water (83.07g) sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. Cholesterol is 0.0 mg — confirmed zero as kiwi is a plant food with no cholesterol by nature (naturally zero). Vitamin B12 is 0.0 mcg and vitamin D is 0.0 mcg — naturally zero as this is a plant-based food. Trans fat is 0.0g — confirmed negligible/zero for raw fruit per USDA. GL per serving calculated using 76g serving size (1 medium peeled kiwi): GL = 52 × (14.66 × 0.76) / 100 ≈ 5.8. No -1 sentinel values require special documentation beyond standard unavailability; all fields populated from approved sources.
